candidates.notes: Multi-layer probe training dramatically improved results. First-letter F1 jumped from 0.083 (pilot L12) to 0.971 (L24) -- a 12x improvement. The previous L12-only pilot was testing the wrong layer. Layer 24 is universally best for all hierarchies. RAVEL probes still below strict 0.90 gate but city-continent at 0.84 is close. The city-country probe at 0.79 with 80 classes is reasonable given class imbalance. Key insight: later layers (18-24) encode factual knowledge as predicted by the methodology.

probe_quality_analysis.layer_trend: Monotonically increasing F1 with layer depth for all hierarchies. Layer 24 > 18 > 12 > 6 consistently.
probe_quality_analysis.first_letter_diagnosis: Previous pilot F1=0.083 at L12 was due to: (1) wrong layer -- first-letter knowledge is at later layers, (2) sae_spelling LinearProbe at L12 position -2 underperforms sklearn at position -1. sklearn at L24 position -1 achieves 0.971.
probe_quality_analysis.method_comparison: sklearn LogReg consistently outperforms sae_spelling LinearProbe at every layer. At L24: sklearn=0.971 vs sae_spelling=0.868. The sklearn approach using position -1 (last token) is more effective than sae_spelling position -2.

failing: city-country L24 (0.789) -- 80 classes with severe imbalance

recommended_actions_for_full_mode: Use L24 for all hierarchies (universally best)
Use sklearn LogReg over sae_spelling LinearProbe
Consider relaxing quality gate to 0.80 for city-country (80 classes)
Try grouping rare countries to reduce class count for city-country
RAVEL prompts can be optimized with fill-in-the-blank templates
